mnemonics: COW: Copy On Write, Don't Worry!
Copy on Write: Only Write when Necessary
keyPoints: Copy-on-write is a technique used in operating systems to avoid page faults when writing to a page that is being accessed by multiple processes.
When a process wants to write to a page, the operating system creates a copy of the page for the process's exclusive use.
This copy is only written to, and any changes are only visible to the process that made the change.
The original page remains the same, and any other processes accessing the page see the original, not the modified copy.
This technique reduces the number of page faults and improves system performance.
